select_when: Novices must absorb several unfamiliar concepts at once and would otherwise confuse the task because too many moving parts arrive together.
A presentation, workflow, lesson, or prompt can be sequenced so attention stays on the next useful chunk instead of the entire problem space.
Stress, ambiguity, or time pressure is already consuming working-memory capacity and decision quality depends on reducing avoidable mental load.
The communication or operating path should be redesigned so the most important distinctions stick instead of being buried in excess detail.
avoid_when: Simplification would remove the causal structure, nuance, or thresholds needed to make a real decision rather than merely creating a feeling of clarity.
Expert users are being forced through novice scaffolding that adds friction, redundancy, or boredom instead of improving performance.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on working-memory constraints, sequencing, and chunk design rather than broad learning-science commentary.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor general UX or product-design overlays because the raw source already gives stronger workload and sequencing structure.
Wave 4 implications stay observational only for this batch.
open_questions: The reasoning-types field is a reviewed taxonomy choice over a source that is more explicit about cognitive mechanism than about mode labels.

select_when.note: The raw source gives explicit activation bullets and nearby context on sequencing, overload, and stress; review mainly compacts them into routing form.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by explicit raw danger lines.

input_type.note: The source is explicit about overloaded workflows and instruction paths, but the machine-usable label is still reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model restructures load through chunking, scaffolding, and sequencing; the output label compresses that into an operational intervention type.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses overload inside larger systems of communication and work, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
